acetaminophen.common_use: Acetaminophen is commonly used for mild to moderate pain and fever.
acetaminophen.general_guidance: Use only as directed on the package label or by your prescriber. Avoid taking more than one product containing acetaminophen at the same time.
side_effects: liver injury if too much is taken
warnings: Ask a clinician or pharmacist before use if you have liver disease or drink alcohol heavily.
Seek urgent help if you may have taken more than directed, even if you feel well.

ibuprofen.common_use: Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used for pain, fever, or inflammation.
ibuprofen.general_guidance: Use the label or prescriber instructions. Taking it with food or milk may reduce stomach upset.
warnings: Ask a clinician before use if you have kidney disease, stomach ulcers, take blood thinners, are pregnant, or were told to avoid NSAIDs.
Seek urgent help for black stools, vomiting blood, swelling of the face or throat, or trouble breathing.

aspirin.common_use: Aspirin may be used for pain or fever and may be prescribed for heart or stroke prevention.
aspirin.general_guidance: Use it only as directed on the label or by your prescriber.
warnings: Do not give aspirin to children or teenagers unless a clinician specifically says to.
Ask a clinician before use if you take blood thinners or have a bleeding disorder.

metformin.common_use: Metformin is prescribed to help manage blood sugar in type 2 diabetes.
metformin.general_guidance: Take it exactly as prescribed and follow your care team's blood sugar monitoring plan.
warnings: Tell your clinician about kidney, liver, heart, or heavy alcohol use risks.
Seek urgent help for severe weakness, unusual sleepiness, deep or rapid breathing, severe vomiting, or feeling very cold.

amoxicillin.common_use: Amoxicillin is an antibiotic used for certain bacterial infections.
amoxicillin.general_guidance: Take it exactly as prescribed and finish the course unless your clinician tells you to stop.
warnings: Get urgent help for swelling of the lips, face, or throat, wheezing, or a severe rash.
Tell your clinician if you have had an allergic reaction to penicillin-type antibiotics.

albuterol.common_use: Albuterol is a quick-relief bronchodilator used for wheezing or breathing symptoms in conditions such as asthma.
albuterol.general_guidance: Use it only according to your asthma action plan or prescriber instructions.
warnings: Seek urgent help if breathing trouble is severe or the rescue inhaler is not helping.
Contact your care team if you need it more often than usual.

cetirizine.common_use: Cetirizine is an antihistamine used for allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes, or hives.
cetirizine.general_guidance: Use as directed on the label or by your clinician.
warnings: Avoid driving or alcohol if it makes you sleepy.
Seek urgent help for trouble breathing or swelling of the face, lips, tongue, or throat.

loratadine.common_use: Loratadine is an antihistamine used for seasonal allergy symptoms and hives.
loratadine.general_guidance: Use as directed on the label or by your clinician.
warnings: Ask a clinician before use if you have liver or kidney disease.
Seek urgent help for breathing trouble or swelling of the face, lips, tongue, or throat.

diphenhydramine.common_use: Diphenhydramine is an antihistamine used for allergy symptoms and sometimes short-term sleep trouble.
diphenhydramine.general_guidance: Use as directed. It can cause strong sleepiness and may not be safe for some older adults or people with certain conditions.
warnings: Avoid alcohol, driving, or other sedating medicines unless a clinician says it is safe.
Ask a clinician before use in older adults, glaucoma, prostate problems, or breathing disease.

omeprazole.common_use: Omeprazole reduces stomach acid and is used for conditions such as heartburn or reflux.
omeprazole.general_guidance: Use as directed on the label or by your prescriber. Persistent symptoms should be reviewed by a clinician.
warnings: Seek urgent help for chest pain, trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, or black stools.
Ask a clinician before long-term use.

lisinopril.common_use: Lisinopril is an ACE inhibitor prescribed for high blood pressure, heart failure, or kidney protection in some people.
lisinopril.general_guidance: Take it exactly as prescribed. Do not stop it without contacting your care team.
warnings: Seek urgent help for swelling of the face, lips, tongue, or throat.
Tell your clinician if you are pregnant, may become pregnant, or have kidney problems.

atorvastatin.common_use: Atorvastatin is a statin prescribed to lower cholesterol and reduce cardiovascular risk.
atorvastatin.general_guidance: Take it as prescribed and keep follow-up lab visits if your clinician ordered them.
warnings: Contact your clinician promptly for severe muscle pain, weakness, dark urine, or yellowing skin or eyes.
Tell your clinician about pregnancy, liver disease, or heavy alcohol use.
